_doc: Per-user partition spec, one entry per contributor (good + bad + freerider + inactive).
Activate by setting partition_strategy='per_user' + per_user_partitions='<this path>'
in ExperimentConfiguration. In per_user mode the spec is the source of truth for
behavior and contributor counts; number_of_*_contributors kwargs are ignored.
Canonical JSON shape (array of users under 'presets'):
{"presets": [{"id": "<guid>", "name": ..., "datasets": {DATASET: spec, ...}}, ...]}
Active dataset is picked from experiment_config.dataset at runtime.
Dataset keys are case-insensitive ('MNIST'/'mnist'/'CIFAR-10'/'cifar-10' all OK).
Legacy shapes still accepted:
{"users": [...]} (array, single- or multi-dataset)
{user_index: spec} (dict, single-dataset only)
Semantics: each user takes a fair stratified share of every class equal to
data_percent of the dataset, then drops samples whose label is not in the
user's whitelist (only_labels or label_distribution.keys()). data_percent
stays a stable mental model regardless of skew. A user with pct=10% and
only_labels=[4,9] effectively trains on ~2% of dataset (2 of 10 classes
kept) -- bump pct if you want full coverage on those classes.
Per-user fields (on each preset entry):
id                 (str, required)    Stable identifier (GUID or numeric string).
Used as user_index for account allocation.
name               (str)              Human-readable label, kept on the user
for logs/plots. Optional.
datasets           (dict)             Dataset name -> spec dict. Each spec dict
carries the per-dataset fields below.
Per-dataset fields (inside each datasets[<name>] block):
data_percent       (float, required)  Fair share of dataset before filtering.
Must be > 0. Sum across users <= 100 per
dataset.
behavior           (str)              One of 'Honest', 'Malicious', 'Free-rider',
'Inactive' (case-insensitive; 'FreeRider'
also OK). Defaults to 'Honest' if missing.
Behavior is per-dataset, so a user can be
honest on MNIST and malicious on CIFAR-10.
Inactive entries are counted toward the
inactive contributor total but never join
the FL round.
noise_scale        (float)            REQUIRED for Malicious/Free-rider. Forbidden
for Honest/Inactive. Per-user noise scale
applied when injecting random weights.
start_round        (int >= 1)         REQUIRED for Malicious/Free-rider. Forbidden
for Honest/Inactive. Round at which the
user switches to its faulty behavior.
only_labels        (list[int])        Hard whitelist. Drops every class outside
the list after fair-share.
label_distribution (dict[int->float]) Per-class retention factor in [0, 1] applied
to the fair share. 1.0 keeps all, 0.5 keeps
half, 0.0 drops the class. Unmentioned
classes default to 1.0.
flip_map           (dict[int->int])   Malicious label-flip map applied at dataset
read time.
Every dataset block must list the same set of preset ids (the participant
roster is fixed across datasets). Behavior, noise_scale and start_round may differ
per dataset. The runner allocates blockchain accounts in sorted-id order, so the
same id set produces the same account assignment across runs.
